observations.recommendation: Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities. Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE,
as this activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets
and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint
custodial responsibilities. .
 Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical
count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the
auditor immediately thereafter.
 Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment
Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property
records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to
reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
a. The municipal treasurer should immediately deposit the cash in vault
amounting to P190,898.34; and
b. Management should follow strictly Section 32, Volume 1 of NGAS (LGUs)
Manual and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collection intact
with the authorized depository bank to avoid exposing the funds from
possible losses through theft

 Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on
Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation
of cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
 Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report
thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books
of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth
day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

We recommended that:
 The municipal accountant should be required to record all consummated
procurement thru the inventory account and properly follow the steps of
recording incoming and out-going supplies and materials in a perpetual
method of inventory keeping. This will prevent purchases in bulk be charged
to outright expense; and
 Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer must maintain Stock Cards as required for
in Section 119 of NGAS Manual Vol. I for local government units.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should direct the procuring department to notify
the Audit Team Leader or any of its representatives on any procurement of
supplies, materials and office equipment consummated for the agency’s
operational purposes during the procuring period within 24 hours after
acceptance thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper
disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation
of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy
programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender
based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a
family.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various
Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology
Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project
requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management should formalize a policy for the granting of
financial assistance to its constituents and the various NGOs taking into
consideration the need and the budget for the year. Beneficiaries should be
properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance
should be extended only once to the same person/ NGO’s.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management requires concerned officials and employees
to support all claims with the required documentations prior to release of
payments / checks to validate disbursements.
The Municipal Accountant should not process transactions/vouchers unless the
required documents are attached.

We recommended that:
 The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement
vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the
Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month.
Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and
other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the
requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section
122(2),P.D. 1445);
 Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent
work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
 Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents,
to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the Municipal Government of Tapul to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that officials concerned and its local Bids and Award Committee personnel to regularly and to adhere to proper internal processes by providing the resident auditor in advance with copies of the Purchase Orders and within 24 hours after acceptance of such procurement inform the assigned audit team leader of the deliveries made.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The municipal treasurer should immediately deposit the cash in vault amounting to P120,398.34; and Management should follow strictly Section 32, Volume 1 of NGAS (LGUs) Manual No. 1 of NGAS (LGUs) Manual and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collection intact with the authorized depository bank to avoid exposing the funds from possible losses through theft or robbery.

Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall faithfully adhere to all the laws and regulation particularly the submission of the annual local disaster risk reduction management investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012-002 to support its legality.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and reported. Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in coordination with the BAC Chairman, facilitate the timely submission of all required documents associated to infrastructure projects implemented by administration. Submit the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS ARMM.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for
the implementation and enforcement of the provision of R.A 9003 within their
respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section
12 and Section 16 of the same Act.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance
through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and
require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the
award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/
programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or
public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this
Office.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the
proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the
implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct
advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater
role for men in raising a family.

Recommendations:

We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the
Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms
issued are properly recorded and reported.

Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and
submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the
NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.

Recommendation:

We recommend that copies of Purchased Orders/Contracts and Acceptance
Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under
Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual,
Volume I.

We recommended that:
a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement
vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the
Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise,
we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other
emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of
the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
c. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to
the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

The municipal treasurer should immediately deposit the cash in vault amounting to P168,273.34; and Management should follow strictly Section 32, Volume 1 of NGAS (LGUs) Manual and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collection intact with the authorized depository bank to avoid exposing the funds from possible losses through theft or robbery.

Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly observe adherence to the provisions of Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (i) the MPDO prepares Annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and annual work and financial plan where disbursements of LDRRMF is strictly based; (ii) transfer to a special trust fund the unexpended LDRRMF for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five year.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein part audit recommendation.

Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance should be extended only once to the same person/ NGO’s.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement
vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the
Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise,
we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other
emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of
the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
c. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to
the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various
Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology
Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project
requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.

The municipal treasurer should immediately deposit the cash in vault amounting to P67,273.34; and Management should follow strictly Section 32, Volume 1 of NGAS (LGUs) Manual and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collection intact with the authorized depository bank to avoid exposing the funds from possible losses through theft or robbery.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that accountable officers that had been drawn must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that thru the Municipal Engineer’s Office in coordination with the BAC Chairman, facilitate the timely submission of all required documents associated to infrastructure projects implemented by administration. Submit the same to the office of the Audit Team Leader for verification, review and enabling for a timely request for technical inspection and validation by the COA-TAS ARMM.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445); Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities. 
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan.  Develop an Action/Implementation Plan.  Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
The municipal accountant should be required to record all consummated procurement thru the inventory account and properly follow the steps of recording incoming and out-going supplies and materials in a perpetual method of inventory keeping.  This will prevent purchases in bulk be charged to outright expense; and
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer must maintain Stock Cards as required for in Section 119 of NGAS Manual Vol. I for local government units.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

Efforts had been made by the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in the coming with timely submission of their required reports of transactions, paid disbursement vouchers/payrolls for recording and for examination and audit.

observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should formalize a policy for the granting of financial assistance to its constituents and the various NGOs taking into consideration the need and the budget for the year.  Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance should be extended only once to the same person/ NGO’s.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end.  Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.

Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Efforts had been made by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer was able to submit all the Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly undertake procurement of commonly-use Goods such as supplies, materials and equipment for the infrastructures and livelihood projects of the Municipality through the Procurement Service (PS)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM) using the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS), in compliance with Section 53 e), IRR-A of RA 9184, except for emergency cases as determined by proper authority and inavailability of items to be procured in the PS-DBM and the amount do not reach the minimum requirement for public bidding, and provided also that the claim for payment shall be supported with approved BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE), that mode of procurement thru shopping shall be allowed,. As such, we also request management to visit PS-DBM Regional Office IX, Zamboanga City for inquiries and/or assistance in order to facilitate undertaking procurement through PS-DBM using PhilGEPS.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.
